title: vepris bachmannii
intro: Vepris bachmannii, also known as the African yellow ironwood, is a species of evergreen tree native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimalarial and antimicrobial activities, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as limonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Vepris bachmannii include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. In traditional herbal medicine, Vepris bachmannii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in the formulation of various herbal products, including teas, capsules, and topical creams.
